Internal Compliance Memo — Reg FD / HIPAA / GLBA guidance for Novalis Care Holdings, Inc. (U.S. issuer listed on a national exchange).
Purpose: reinforce disclosure controls for healthcare and banking workflows during analyst interactions, employee briefings, and securities communications.
As a reminder, the company’s Q1 operating metrics were publicly announced via press release and Form 8-K on May 2; use only those public figures in external discussions.
Regulation FD bars selective disclosure to sell-side analysts, lenders, or buy-side clients; direct all Q&A to prepared remarks and posted FAQs.
Rule 10b-5 prohibits trading on or tipping material non-public information; when in doubt, route to the disclosure committee.
Do not include form fields like Name: ______, SSN: ___-__-____, Address: __________________ in any investor or lender materials.
Healthcare data: patient encounters may be discussed only in de-identified, aggregated form; e.g., regional volumes already disclosed in the investor deck.
Banking workflows: HSA onboarding examples must be synthetic; do not transmit Account No: 00000000 or Routing: 000000000 (invalid) outside secure test environments.
Company identifiers shown in drafts (EIN 00-0000000; State Reg No. ST-000-0000) are placeholders and must not appear in public filings.
Send all investor inquiries to investorrelations@novaliscare.example and media inquiries to press@novaliscare.example; do not use personal emails.
For privacy and ethics questions, call 800-555-0143 (privacy helpline) or 800-555-0176 (ethics helpline); these are general help lines for policy guidance.
Travel compliance has cleared U.S. Passport No. 518228764 for Employee 47 solely for visa scheduling; do not retain this number beyond the trip file.
Address examples such as 123 Any Street, Suite 000, City, ST 00000 are training-only and should never be copied into transaction documents.
If asked about FY guidance beyond what appears in the May 2 public materials, decline and refer the requester to our website and filed 8-K.
Preliminary lab outcomes, unannounced payer contracts, draft capital ratios, or internal target ranges constitute MNPI and must not be discussed outside the disclosure committee.
Questions from regulators or the exchange should be routed to Legal at legal@novaliscare.example; employees should not respond individually.
